_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Keep-alive scheduler re-fires successful slots around UTC midnight (TZ mismatch in get_due_slots)

steps.description: Replace the `func.date(KeepAliveRun.fired_at) == today_date` filter in `get_due_slots` (orch/keep_alive_service.py) with a TIMESTAMPTZ half-open range filter using tz-aware local-midnight bounds. Build today_start_local/tomorrow_start_local from the system's local timezone (datetime.now().astimezone().tzinfo) so the bounds are unambiguous regardless of the DB session's TimeZone setting. Do not touch any other function, file, or call site.
Review S01: predicate correctness (half-open range, tz-aware bounds, no off-by-one), no other code touched, SQLAlchemy 2.0 idiom preserved, lint/format gate clean.

Add bug-exposing integration test + tz-offset parametrized variants in tests/integration/test_keep_alive_integration.py. Each variant freezes Python's clock and asserts get_due_slots(db) returns [] after a successful run is logged. Use the testcontainer db_session fixture; no live-DB connections, no DB mocks.
Review S03: RED evidence captured, assertions verify behaviour (empty list, no fire), parametrized tz coverage, testcontainer isolation, no order-dependence under pytest-randomly.
Cross-step global review: AC1/AC2/AC3 traceability, predicate <-> test consistency, scope adherence (only files in scope.allowed_paths changed), no regression to the existing keep_alive tests.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
